title: Tracey Nelson Announces New Album Hercules Produced By MJ Lenderman & Featuring Wednesday
author: Tom Breihan
summary:

The post Tracey Nelson Announces New Album <em>Hercules</em> Produced By MJ Lenderman & Featuring Wednesday appeared first on Stereogum.
full_text:
Singer-songwriter Tracey Nelson is based in New York, but he's evidently friends with all the countrified indie rockers of Asheville, North Carolina. Last month, Nelson signed to the new K Records imprint Perennial and shared "Dolly's Coat," a collaboration with the very busy MJ Lenderman. Today, Nelson announces his album Hercules, and Lenderman produced and played on the entire thing. 

It's not just Lenderman, either. Hercules, which originally came out on CD-R, also has contributions from Wednesday members Karly Hartzman, Ethan Baechtold, and Xandy Chelmis. Colin Miller and Landon George, two members of Lenderman's band, are on the album as well, as is former Hotline TNT guitarist Jack Kraus. Before you ask, no, I don't know what's up with that cover art.

"Dolly's Coat" is on Hercules, and so is "Just Shoot Me Now," a song that Nelson quietly dropped on YouTube last year. (As I write this, the video has fewer than a thousand views, so it's probably just as new to you as it is to me. It's really good.) Today, Nelson shares the album-opening title track from "Hercules." As with those other two songs, it's a blissfully melancholic folk-rock amble that fits very cozily with Lenderman, Wednesday, and that whole crew. Below, check out the song's video, directed by Jolie M-A, and the album's tracklist.

TRACKLIST:
01 "Hercules"
02 "86" 
03 "Two Feet "
04 "The Can"
05 "That's More Like a Lover"
06 "St. John's River"
07 "Pedro's Joint"
08 "Green Shag"
09 "Fighting With The Wind"
10 "Dear Brothers"
11 "Chandelier"
12 "Just Shoot Me Now"
13 "Dolly's Coat"

Hercules is out 7/10 on Perennial/K.
